publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:40:57 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07879John D'Emilio papers7879Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:06:20 UTC. >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
John D'Emilio papers
D'Emilio, John
7879
7 cubic feet.
1990-2003 Research files from John D'Emilio's book "Lost prophet: the life and times of Bayard Rustin". <<<<<<< HEAD 8 (unprocessed) 9 (unprocessed) 10 (unprocessed) ======= 8 (unprocessed) 9 (unprocessed) 10 (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 11064312
Content Description
Research files from John D'Emilio's book "Lost prophet: the life and times of Bayard Rustin".
Arrangement
Folder titles were recorded as they appeared on the original physical folders, which were divised by the creator of the collection. In cases where folders were labeled "BRINTER", processing staff recorded the title as "Bayard Rustin interview". Folders recorded as "Untitled" were originally left blank by the creator or contained material that was found loose in the box.
Related Materials
Material in this collection was used to create "Lost prophet, the life and times of Bayard Rustin", E185.97.R93 D46 2003.
Preferred Citation
John D'Emilio papers, #7879. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
John D'Emilio (born 1948) is a professor emeritus of history and of women's and gender studies at the University of Illinois at Chicago where he taught 1999-2014. He first taught at the University of North Carolina at Greensboro (1983-1993). He earned his Ph.D. from Columbia University in 1982.
In 1989, he joined the Advisory Committee for Cornell's new Human Sexuality Collection and helped shape its direction and success. He served as Founding Director of the Policy Institute at the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force (now named the National LGBTQ Task Force) from 1995 to 1997. He was a National Endowment for the Humanities fellow in 1997 and a Guggenheim fellow in 1998.
Among his publications are Intimate Matters: A History of Sexuality in America [with Estelle Freedman], Lost Prophet: The Life and Times of Bayard Rustin; Queer Legacies: Stories from Chicago's LGBTQ Archives; and two important collections of essays: Making Trouble: "Essays on Gay History, Politics, and the University", and "The World Turned: Essays on Gay History, Politics, and Culture".
African Americans -- Biography. Civil rights workers -- United States -- Biography African American pacifists African Americans -- Civil rights -- History -- 20th century Civil rights movements. Nonviolence Gay authors College Teachers--United States History of sexuality Gay rights Rustin, Bayard, 1912-1987
